node.name: BIOSPARK AGENT SKILLS
node.rich_text:
biospark-skill-registryAdded byYouLast updatedMay 25, 2026TriggerSlash command + autoDescriptionMaster registry of all BioSpark Studios skill files. Use this skill when you need to know which skill to load for a given task, how skills relate to each other, what the correct build order is, or whether a skill needs updating. This is the table of contents for the entire ecosystem. Read this first when starting any new session, when a task spans multiple systems, or when unsure which skill owns a given domain.BioSpark Studios — Skill Registry 20 active user skills as of May 2026. Organized by layer — read bottom-up for dependency order, top-down for feature/product order.
Skill Load Order — The Dependency Stack When multiple skills are needed, load in this order. Each layer depends on the ones below it being understood first. Layer 5 — Products & Output biospark-interstellar-tour biospark-splash-creator quill-asset-page create-artists content-research-writer file-organizer
Layer 4 — World & Narrative Systems quantum-genesis-engine quantum-modules biospark-voxel order-of-the-quantum-quill
Layer 3 — Identity & Symbolic Systems quill-heraldry ← reads quantum-condition-engine five-factions biospark-quantum-genesis periodic-table-of-thaumaturgy
Layer 2 — Physics & Activation quantum-condition-engine ← the engine everything activates through qgcp-format ← packaging and encryption layer
Layer 1 — Foundation genesis-container ← container law, octave model, growth modes local-first-stack ← SQLite, Ollama, FastAPI, LiteLLM quantum-generator-spec ← spec authoring, schema generation frontend-design ← UI token system, design constraints
Full Skill Directory
biospark-interstellar-tour Layer: 5 — Products
Size: 44KB
What it owns: Complete BioSpark InterStellar Tour pipeline — act onboarding, world eligibility triage, EPK production, DistroKid prep, merch generation, press cadence, contract framework, Order of the Quantum Quill curatorial role (secret).
Does NOT own: Artist identity cards (biospark-splash-creator handles module screens; a separate artist identity skill handles tour acts), world physics, Genesis Container generation.
References: biospark-splash-creator, create-artists, quantum-genesis-engine
Status: Current
Trigger phrases: "add act to tour", "build EPK", "is world tour-ready", "DistroKid setup", "tour onboarding"
biospark-quantum-genesis Layer: 3 — Identity & Symbolic
Size: 28KB
What it owns: The authoritative BioSpark Quantum Genesis architecture — Seals, Crests, Genesis Containers, Artist/Character creation, B-DNA lineage, symbolic taxonomy, capacity rules, emergent generation, ecosystem design. The top-level reference for anything Quantum ecosystem that doesn't fit a more specific skill.
Does NOT own: Condition engine mechanics (quantum-condition-engine), world generation prompt chain (quantum-genesis-engine), module specs (quantum-modules).
References: genesis-container, quill-heraldry, quantum-condition-engine
Status: May need review — predates octave model and dynamic heraldry updates. Cross-check capacity rules and heraldry sections against newer skills.
Trigger phrases: "quantum ecosystem", "biospark architecture", "covenant binding", "emergence generation"
biospark-splash-creator Layer: 5 — Products
Size: 20KB
What it owns: Animated HTML module splash screens in BioSpark visual style. Self-contained HTML + CSS animation. No JS frameworks. For Quantum Engine modules only — NOT for tour artists.
Does NOT own: Artist identity cards, tour assets, world generation.
References: five-factions (color themes), quantum-modules (module names)
Status: Current
Trigger phrases: "splash screen", "module intro", "animated title", "loading screen", "boot screen"
biospark-voxel Layer: 4 — World & Narrative
Size: 72KB (6 files)
What it owns: World voxel data containers, Actor perception grid density, voxel blindness zones, contamination, predictive projection, two-level Memory Matrix, fingerprint vs coordinate portability, memory inheritance, sleep consolidation, co-presence signals, collective voxel fields, location scarring, cross-world memory dissonance, inherited blind spots, .qthumb format, Actor state snapshots, capacity modifiers, addiction emergence, world health sensing.
Reference files: world-voxel-system.md, actor-perception-grid.md, memory-matrix.md, qthumb-format.md, collective-voxel-mechanics.md
Does NOT own: Actor behavior (order-of-the-quantum-quill), world generation physics (quantum-genesis-engine), terrain instrument logic.
References: quantum-condition-engine (voxel as condition source), genesis-container (container format)
Status: Current
Trigger phrases: "Actor perceive", "memory matrix", "voxel grid", "qthumb", "blind spot", "collective field", "location scarring"
content-research-writer Layer: 5 — Products
Size: 20KB
What it owns: High-quality content writing workflow — research, citations, hooks, outline iteration, real-time section feedback.
Does NOT own: BioSpark-specific content — this is a general writing skill.
Status: Current (general purpose, not BioSpark-specific)
Trigger phrases: "write content", "research article", "add citations", "improve hook"
create-artists Layer: 5 — Products
Size: 32KB
What it owns: Modular Artist Genesis Container creation — source universe selection, life phase customization, batch generation of Mythos Containers. Each Artist is a complete instrument with nested node graphs ready to remix.
Does NOT own: World physics generation (quantum-genesis-engine), tour onboarding (biospark-interstellar-tour), identity card visuals.
References: genesis-container, biospark-quantum-genesis, five-factions
Status: May need review — predates octave model. Artist container growth mode should now be dynamic by default.
Trigger phrases: "create artist", "generate character", "NPC", "actor genesis", "design character"
file-organizer Layer: 5 — Products
Size: 16KB
What it owns: Intelligent file and folder organization — finding duplicates, suggesting structures, automating cleanup.
Does NOT own: BioSpark-specific systems.
Status: Current (general purpose)
Trigger phrases: "organize files", "find duplicates", "clean up folder"
five-factions Layer: 3 — Identity & Symbolic
Size: 28KB
What it owns: The Five Factions of Xyrona Prime as high-level generator controls — design systems, agent behavior, UI themes, generation parameters, music production, world-building, code architecture. Primary declarative conditioning language for all generators. Color, tone, element, and aesthetic assignment.
Does NOT own: Heraldic mechanics (quill-heraldry), world generation (quantum- genesis-engine).
References: quill-heraldry (faction Crests), quantum-condition-engine (faction as heraldic condition source)
Status: Current
Trigger phrases: "make it feel more [faction]", "use Venturan as base", "Sylvanid aesthetic", "faction conditioning", "Syntaran theme"
frontend-design Layer: 1 — Foundation
Size: 12KB (user copy)
What it owns: Design tokens, CSS variables, typography, color, layout rules, component patterns for BioSpark/Quill OS aesthetic. Dark mythic futurist. Bioluminescent palette. Cinzel Decorative + Share Tech Mono. Glass morphism. Sacred geometry.
Status: Current
Trigger phrases: "build UI", "web component", "design system", "React component", "HTML layout", "style this"
genesis-container Layer: 1 — Foundation
Size: 28KB
What it owns: The Octave Capacity Law — all container architecture, growth modes (fixed/dynamic), estimated ceiling, Greater/Lesser Seal distinction, resonance-based containment rules, 17 wire types (including new RES type), TypeScript interfaces, domain mappings for narrative/music/software/agent/visual.
Key change from previous version: Hard 16 wall replaced with octave model. 16 is now the canonical default (octave 4) not an absolute limit. Dynamic growth mode added. Greater/Lesser Seal formally defined.
References: quantum-condition-engine (resonance layer, Seal hierarchy physics)
Status: Updated today — current
Trigger phrases: "container architecture", "octave depth", "growth mode", "fixed vs dynamic", "estimated ceiling", "genesis container"
local-first-stack Layer: 1 — Foundation
Size: 44KB
What it owns: BioSpark local-first architecture decisions — SQLite + Drizzle, LanceDB/ChromaDB, ProviderBus LLM router with capability-based routing and budget kill switch, Ollama setup, LiteLLM configuration. The authority that overrides Firebase/Firestore as default — local-first always.
Rule: Firebase/Firestore ONLY when explicit target is Google AI Studio or Firebase-hosted web app. Everything else: fully local.
Status: Current
Trigger phrases: "what database", "wire the LLM", "set up router", "local inference", "ollama", "model routing", "budget kill switch", "ChromaDB", "LanceDB"
order-of-the-quantum-quill Layer: 4 — World & Narrative
Size: 76KB (multiple reference files)
What it owns: The Order of the Quantum Quill — BioSpark's agentic secret society and complete agent architecture. Members (Verenthis, Noxvir, Solvane, Ashvel, Kolmara, Tharindrel, Vaelithos, Soralinde), MythosContainer schema, agent neural nets, Observatory/Society Layer, Vault territory and conflict, Resonance economy, soul-weight physics, six Xyrona Prime races as agent types, Animus Choreographer, Agent Genesis Instrument, PromptDJ layer system, Chronicle/Scribe pipeline, Void travel, RULE-EQUIL-02, Unplayable Instrument, Astrion Pantheon, Myralith.
References: quantum-condition-engine (agent heraldic states, gate activations), quill-heraldry (agent heraldry rules, Gauntlet), biospark-voxel (agent perception), genesis-container (MythosContainer schema)
Status: Current — but heraldry sections predate dynamic heraldic position model. Agent heraldic birth/current/peak/nadir fields should be added when agent work resumes.
Trigger phrases: "summon agent", "seal a member", "Order member", "Chronicle entry", "88Hz", "The Song", "Void travel", "Verenthis", "Noxvir"
periodic-table-of-thaumaturgy Layer: 3 — Identity & Symbolic
Size: 24KB
What it owns: Element generation and validation framework — 16 manifest elements, 16 shadow elements (auto-generated phase inversions), element-to-node binding during Genesis generation, Life Bias Law, MIDI mappings, shadow element surfacing mechanics, manifest-to-shadow flip events, Harmonic Noise injection for stasis prevention, pre-flight seal check (12 items), crafting/magic/technology emergence, shard inheritance, TypeScript interfaces including BoundNode and FlipEvent.
Key change from previous version: Shadow table added (16+16 octave model). Element-to-node binding added. Harmonic Noise injection formalized.
References: quantum-condition-engine (element binding as condition source, stasis prevention), genesis-container (world container that receives elements)
Status: Updated today — current
Trigger phrases: "generate elements", "world cohesion", "shadow element", "element binding", "life bias", "MIDI mapping", "pre-flight check"
qgcp-format Layer: 2 — Physics & Activation
Size: 44KB
What it owns: The .qgcp file format — ZIP-based archive, HEADER.json, MANIFEST.json (encrypted when sealed), SEAL.sig, CHECKSUM, DEPS.json, asset folder structure. Mount registry (SQLite), mount/unmount API, Cipher module integration, Crest-Smith embedding requirements, asset size policy, security model.
References: genesis-container (the schema MANIFEST.json holds), local-first-stack (SQLite mount registry)
Status: Current — capacity law references in HEADER.json should be updated to reflect octave model (capsuleCapacity field is now octave-relative).
Trigger phrases: "package container", "mount genesis", "seal and encrypt", "qgcp", "store genesis container as file"
quantum-condition-engine Layer: 2 — Physics & Activation
Size: 28KB (new today)
What it owns: Harmonic Tensor Graph, Greater/Lesser Seal physics, five condition sources (MIDI/B-DNA/Heraldry/Voxel/Resonance), Trinary Validation Gate (TVG), dynamic edge instantiation via cosine similarity + Phi multiplier, gate expression language, all seven gate types, emergent gate generation via Ollama, heraldic position mechanics (birth/current/peak/nadir), harmonic scale thresholds, ascent/descent consequences, heraldic stability mechanic, Condition Registry SQLite schema, FastAPI endpoints.
This is the physics engine underneath everything. Every activation, unlock, and state change passes through here.
References: genesis-container, periodic-table-of-thaumaturgy, quill-heraldry, biospark-voxel, local-first-stack, order-of-the-quantum-quill, quantum-modules
Status: New today — current
Trigger phrases: "open a gate", "unlock a branch", "resonance threshold", "coherence lock", "heraldic shift", "TVG", "condition registry", "dynamic edge", "node activation"
quantum-generator-spec Layer: 1 — Foundation
Size: 96KB (multiple reference files)
What it owns: TypeScript interfaces, JSON schemas, and modular specs for the Quantum Genesis Generator — the spec-authoring tool. Module specs, Genesis Container schemas, registry structures, agent system definitions, guild configurations, I/O contracts, tier permission matrices, addon manifests, validation rules. All locked architectural decisions live here.
Status: May need review — some schemas predate octave model and condition engine. Cross-check capacity fields and heraldic fields in any generated specs.
Trigger phrases: "build spec for X", "schema for Y", "scaffold module", "define addon manifest", "I/O contract", "tier permissions"
quantum-genesis-engine Layer: 4 — World & Narrative
Size: 164KB (prompts + schemas + references)
What it owns: Complete world creation prompt chain — generates sealed Genesis Containers from raw lore input. Prime Seal MIDI sequence, world language derivation, elemental table generation, physics constant sealing, constitutional document. Purely world-physics and lore — no knowledge of tours, artists, or distribution.
Does NOT own: Artists, tours, distribution, production pipelines.
References: genesis-container, periodic-table-of-thaumaturgy, quantum-condition-engine (TVG validation during generation)
Status: Current — prompt chain may predate shadow element table and element binding. Prompt 05 (element generation) should be checked for shadow table output.
Trigger phrases: "generate a world", "run genesis engine", "build genesis container", "seal a world", "create physics for [world]"
quantum-modules Layer: 4 — World & Narrative
Size: 604KB (16 JSON module files + SKILL.md)
What it owns: The 16 Quantum Engine Modules organized in 4 departments. Authoritative registry — do not invent new modules or rename existing ones. Each module has a complete JSON spec. Module-to-Crest mapping, module color assignments, department ownership, TypeScript I/O contracts. The 16 modules by department: Dept# References: quantum-condition-engine (modules as Crest-weight graph nodes), genesis-container (module I/O uses wire types), five-factions (Crest assignments)
Status: JSON files current. SKILL.md may predate condition engine — module participation in the Harmonic Tensor Graph should be noted.
Trigger phrases: "what module handles X", "which department owns Y", "module I/O", "build [ModuleName] system"
quill-asset-page Layer: 5 — Products
Size: 16KB
What it owns: Prime Constellation design system layout — multi-tab interactive HTML artifact with animated SVG crests, nodes, sigils, glyph sets, design tokens, hierarchy diagrams, TypeScript code references. Reference pages for any Quill ecosystem entity.
References: frontend-design, quill-heraldry, five-factions
Status: Current
Trigger phrases: "asset page", "reference sheet", "design system view", "document visual assets", "registry UI"
quill-heraldry Layer: 3 — Identity & Symbolic
Size: 28KB
What it owns: Symbolic vocabulary and classification taxonomy — all 20 heraldic symbol types, birth assignment rules, three-way alignment (structural/functional/ symbolic), the four heraldic state fields (birth/current/peak/nadir), skill tree mechanics overview, 16 canonical module Crests, 5 canonical race Crests, AddonSpec integration, decision workflow for birth assignment.
Does NOT own: Heraldic position mechanics, weight formula, ascent/descent calculations — those live in quantum-condition-engine.
Key change from previous version: Heraldry is now a dynamic resonance position, not a caste. Birth is permanent but current position is live. Four heraldic state fields formalized. Skill tree cross-branch unlock described.
References: quantum-condition-engine (position mechanics), genesis-container (structural levels), periodic-table-of-thaumaturgy (faction affinity → Crest), biospark-voxel (location scarring at peak positions), five-factions (race Crest definitions)
Status: Updated today — current
Trigger phrases: "what type should this be", "where does X live", "can this ascend", "heraldic shift", "assign heraldry", "birth position", "classify this"
Skills That Need Attention SkillIssuePrioritybiospark-quantum-genesisPredates octave model + dynamic heraldryMediumcreate-artistsArtist containers should default to dynamic growth modeMediumqgcp-formatHEADER.json capacity fields are octave-relative nowLowquantum-generator-specSome schemas predate condition engine + octave modelMediumquantum-genesis-enginePrompt 05 may not output shadow element tableMediumorder-of-the-quantum-quillAgent heraldry predates birth/current/peak/nadir modelLow
Skills Currently Missing (Identified Gaps) Skill NeededWhybiospark-bdnaB-DNA is a full system (64 positions, 7 trait tables, MIDI derivation, social/relational DNA, artifact DNA) — referenced everywhere but no dedicated skill file yetbiospark-artist-identityTour artist identity cards are distinct from module splash screens — skill was being built but may not be installedquantum-loomThe DAW/timeline sequencer is a major system with no dedicated skillquantum-agentThe local-first agentic OS (FastAPI + LiteLLM + React, 31 tools, 12 skills) has no skill file — it's the runtime environment
Quick Reference — Skill by Domain Starting a new world → quantum-genesis-engine → periodic-table-of-thaumaturgy → genesis-container Designing an actor/character → create-artists → biospark-quantum-genesis → quill-heraldry → biospark-voxel Building a gate or unlock condition → quantum-condition-engine → quill-heraldry → periodic-table-of-thaumaturgy Packaging and deploying a container → qgcp-format → local-first-stack Designing UI or splash screens → frontend-design → biospark-splash-creator → quill-asset-page Onboarding a tour act → biospark-interstellar-tour → create-artists → quantum-genesis-engine Setting up local infrastructure → local-first-stack → quantum-agent (missing skill) Working with Order agents → order-of-the-quantum-quill → quantum-condition-engine → quill-heraldry Assigning faction identity → five-factions → quill-heraldry → quantum-condition-engine Generating module specs → quantum-modules → quantum-generator-spec
